page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-48-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the c4d family. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs and 378.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of approximately 7.88 GB of RAM per core. The instance operates with dedicated CPU allocation and does not include GPU hardware accelerators.

Although the instance family designation includes local solid-state drive references, the specific local storage capacity is provisioned as none, requiring external block storage attachments for persistent data. The configuration includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses, meaning network architectures must utilize alternative routing or address translation mechanisms for external connectivity.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes this instance class suitable for memory-intensive workloads without the overhead of excessive compute cores. It is designed for workloads such as medium-sized databases, in-memory caching layers, enterprise applications, and memory-bound data processing tasks that require dedicated CPU execution.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-48-lssd is a memory-optimized server instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs and 378.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 7.88 GB per vCPU. The instance does not include integrated GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, and local storage is configured as none, necessitating network-attached storage. This hardware profile is engineered for memory-intensive applications, such as in-memory databases, caching systems, and enterprise applications requiring dedicated compute resources. It offers a balanced resource allocation for workloads that demand substantial memory capacity without requiring a proportional increase in CPU cores, optimizing resource density and operational efficiency.

tagline: A dedicated x86_64 compute instance featuring high memory density and forty-eight virtual cores for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

Zero integrated GPUs and no local storage allocation
Designed for in-memory databases and caching layers
Requires external block storage and network configuration for public IPv4 routing
